---
title: "Figure 4—figure supplement 2 — R against IR, all five parameters: the information distortion is diffuse for R, confined for IR"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
SECOND MOMENT. This is the distortion half of the all-parameters R-vs-IR comparison; the bias half is
supplement 1. Split because each moment independently makes the same claim (see supplement 1).

Measured, fraction of (interval, noise, N_ch) distortion cells departing by >= 15%:
                 IR      R
    k_on        2.4%   52%
    k_off        11%   68%
    i             0%   27%
    N_ch        3.6%   38%
    sigma_noise   0%    0%

IR's distortion is CONFINED to k_off and N_ch (and vanishes with N_ch); R's is DIFFUSE across four of
five. Only the instrumental-noise direction is clean for both, which is the shared-null control row:
it says the machinery is not simply painting everything. Conditioning on the interval LOCALISES the
distortion. This justifies Figure 4's choice of k_off and N_ch by showing what it leaves out, and it
is a claim R could have refuted and did not. Supplement 4 shows that what survives for R in every one
of these directions is the correlation part.

DATA: battery_pool_G (theta_pool). Gaussian family. Standard 4 N_ch x 3 noise grid. No new runs.

UNIDENTIFIED: R's k_on and N_ch at the ill-conditioned N_ch 10000, Delta = 1 corner (kappa ~ 66000)
deflate below the 0.5 floor and are drawn grey (value off scale AND kappa > 3e4), the same near-null
corner as the bias supplement. Not a scale too tight for the bulk; add_unident in
figure_4_supp_common.R marks them.

TRAP: the "noise" label is not the physical noise. current_noise = label/1000.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
source("figure_4_supp_common.R")
```

```{r data}
kap  <- read_kappa()
stat <- readcell("battery_pool_G") %>%
  filter(component_path == DIST, param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX,
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  shape(ci_toward = 1) %>%
  add_unident(min(DIST_LIN), max(DIST_LIN), kap)   # grey where clamped AND ill-conditioned
cat("supp2 (distortion):", nrow(CELLS), "cells, all five parameters\n")
cat("scale saturation:\n"); sat(log10(stat$conf), min(DIST_BREAKS), max(DIST_BREAKS), "distortion")
cat("greyed (unidentified) cells:", sum(stat$unident), "->",
    paste(unique(with(filter(stat, unident), paste(ALGO_LAB[algo], param, "N_ch", Num_ch))), collapse = "; "), "\n")
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 9.9}
nr <- length(PARAM_ORD) * length(ALGOS)      # 10 rows
gC <- mapblock(stat, function(d) log10(d$conf), DIST_PAL, DIST_BREAKS)
g <- gC / colorbar(DIST_PAL, DIST_LAB, "distortion", DIST_LIN, sol = c(0.87, 1.15), dsh = c(0.67, 1.5)) +
     plot_layout(heights = c(0.95 * nr, 0.3))
H <- 0.95 * nr + 0.3 + 0.5
g
ggsave("Figure_4_supplement_2.pdf", g, width = 7.0, height = H)
cat("Figure_4_supplement_2.pdf:", nr, "rows, height", round(H, 1), "in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
cat("\n=== supp 2 (distortion) caption numbers ===\n")
cat("\nfraction of cells departing >= 15%, and median, per parameter and algorithm\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% group_by(param, algo) %>%
  summarise(frac_15 = mean(abs(conf - 1) >= 0.15), median = median(conf), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>% arrange(match(param, PARAM_ORD), algo)),
  digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
```
